channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Murang'a Governor Announces Run for 2027 Election on DCP Ticket
item.description: Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata has declared his intention to contest the 2027 gubernatorial election on the Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) ticket, led by former Deputy President Rigathi Gac...

item.title: Alas Men Relegated to 5th Place in SEA V Cup After Loss to Indonesia
item.description: The Alas Pilipinas Men's volleyball team has been relegated to a 5th-place match in the SEA V Cup after suffering a defeat to Indonesia. This marks the second consecutive time the team has been placed...

item.title: Tanzania Insight News Update: July 24, 2026
item.description: The provided text appears to be a placeholder or incomplete webpage content from Tanzania Insight, a news platform. It includes repeated mentions of 'Array July 24, 2026' alongside social media icons ...

item.title: PMD Issues Weather Advisory: Monsoon Rain and Thunderstorms Expected Across Pakistan from July 24-28
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a weather advisory predicting widespread monsoon rain and thunderstorms across the country from July 24 to 28. The forecast highlights heavy rai...

item.title: Ramaphosa's Legal Battle Over Alleged Stolen Cash Stalls As Court Halts Impeachment Probe
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has successfully asked a South African court to pause impeachment proceedings against him over allegations of hiding $580,000 in stolen cash at his rural home. The Western Ca...

item.title: Marais Erasmus's Book Explores Cricket Umpiring and Life Beyond the Field
item.description: The article details the life and career of Marais Erasmus, a renowned cricket umpire, through his newly published book. Erasmus, known for his dynamic personality and rock-and-roll attitude, reflects ...

item.title: Lawyer Claims Southport Killer Attempting to Manipulate System via Psychiatric Transfer
item.description: Axel Rudakubana, 19, the convicted murderer of three children in Southport, has been transferred from HMP Belmarsh to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital. His lawyer, Chris Walker, alleges this move is an ...

item.title: Hospitality industry's gender imbalance highlighted at Brisbane food festival
item.description: The article discusses the gender imbalance in Brisbane's hospitality industry, highlighted by an all-male chef lineup at the James Street Food and Wine Trail festival. Despite the event's focus on sho...

item.title: Montreal Man Faces Trial in New York for Daughter's Murder
item.description: Luciano Frattolin, a Montreal man accused of murdering his nine-year-old daughter Melina, is facing a trial in New York State. The case, which resumed on July 24, 2026, involves charges of second-degr...

item.title: Expert Warns of Severe Consequences from Potential U.S.-Iran Escalation
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's recent threats to launch a massive attack on Iran have raised alarms among experts, who warn of catastrophic humanitarian and strategic consequences. Political scientist ...

item.title: Delhi Govt Extends Mobile Internet Suspension Around Jantar Mantar Till Midnight
item.description: The Delhi government has extended the mobile internet shutdown in and around the Jantar Mantar protest site until 12 midnight, marking the sixth such order issued in the area over five days. The lates...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi alleges pellet gun use during Jantar Mantar protests, cites student injuries
item.description: Rahul Gandhi, Congress leader and Lok Sabha Opposition leader, alleged that pellet guns were used against peaceful student protesters in Delhi during the Jantar Mantar protests. He presented a student...

item.title: Political Prisoners Oppose Mass Transfers Due to Health and Legal Concerns
item.description: A petition signed by 45 political prisoners, including 12 senior citizens and 18 with medical conditions, has been filed with the Department of Justice (DOJ) urging it to stop mass transfers of person...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Hearings into TRC Cases
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, tasked with investigating alleged efforts to delay the prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators, is holding hearings in Newtown, Johannesburg. The commission's wor...

item.title: Economic Insights from Novel Indicators Like the Oasis Index
item.description: The article explores how unconventional metrics such as the Oasis index, which tracks the cost of five beers and two cigarette packs in cities worldwide, provide meaningful economic insights. While se...

item.title: Dennis Mchanga: Blending Animation and Storytelling to Preserve Tanzanian History
item.description: Dennis Mchanga is a Tanzanian animator who uses his craft to preserve history and reclaim cultural identity. Unlike traditional animators focused on cartoons, he blends filmmaking and animation to tel...

item.title: Delhi and Bombay High Courts Establish Fast-Track Courts for Paper Leak Cases Following Modi's Directive
item.description: The Delhi and Bombay High Courts have established special fast-track courts to address cases related to question paper leaks and other malpractices in public examinations. This decision follows Prime ...

item.title: Pakistan, Iran, and US Commit to Strengthen Ties Under Islamabad MoU to Ensure Regional Peace
item.description: Pakistan, Iran, and the United States have emphasized the importance of maintaining close coordination and dialogue to achieve lasting regional peace and stability. During a meeting between Deputy Pri...

item.title: Parent-Child Interaction Therapy: Enhancing Parenting Skills and Child Behavior
item.description: Parent-child interaction therapy (PCIT) is an evidence-based approach that helps parents manage challenging behaviors in children by fostering authoritative parenting. The therapy, rooted in attachmen...

item.title: Carney's Initiative to Attract $1 Trillion in Foreign Investment Over Two Days
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is spearheading the Canada Investment Summit, aiming to reverse a $1 trillion loss in foreign capital over the past decade. The Royal Bank of Canada reported that for every ...
